description_long: This traffic barricade is constructed from a series of rectangular, blocky beams arranged in a sawhorse style, with two horizontal planks supported by four vertical legs. The main visual feature is the bold diagonal striping in alternating yellow and black, which covers both horizontal planks and serves as a high-visibility warning pattern. The structure appears to be made of a solid, possibly plastic or wood-like material, with a pixelated or voxelated design aesthetic. The legs are evenly spaced and provide stability, while the overall form is lightweight and portable, suitable for temporary placement in urban environments. There are no visible lights, signage, or additional fixtures attached to the barricade. The object is designed to act as a visual and physical barrier to restrict or redirect pedestrian or vehicular movement.
description: A yellow and black striped sawhorse-style traffic barricade with four blocky legs and two horizontal planks, designed for high visibility and temporary access control.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full face of the barricade with bold diagonal yellow and black stripes and both horizontal planks clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the barricade, with the legs and planks aligned, making the structure appear narrow.
description_view_2: Rear view, visually identical to the front, displaying the same striped pattern and structural details.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile and the alignment of the legs and planks.
attribute_reflective_striping_pattern: Diagonal stripes, alternating yellow and black, medium width, repeated across both horizontal planks.
